results.title: Alternative Approaches to Addressing Non-Normal Distributions in the Application of IRT Models to Personality Measures
results.content: ## , or oversampling individuals from low trait ranges. Alternatively, we can reconceptualize the construct with a corresponding change in model and assumptions. In the following two sections, we consider two alternative modeling strategies. [...] alternative IRT models and illustrate their strengths and weaknesses, we performed real data application comparing results to those from a graded response model. We review both statistical and theoretical challenges in applying these models and choosing among them. Future applications of these and other alternative models (e.g., unfolding, diffusion) are needed to advance understanding about model choice in particular situations. [...] and to explore the application of alternative models. Only then can we obtain additional substantive insights and findings from these models.

results.title: Are there any other apps as good as Nomi? : r/NomiAI
results.content:
If you're into roleplaying and world building, infiniteworlds.app is the best i found. It's the nearest to having a TTRPG DM / GameMaster you can get in AI. I used up about 60 USD in 1 week, it's crazy expensive. Somedays i play 6-8 hours straight. The world is alive, and events happens behind the scene, there's a "secretinfo" mechanic where things happened and you're unaware of it, just like a DM planning things behind the DM panel.

 Public 

Anyone can view, post, and comment to this community

0 0

## Top Posts

   Reddit reReddit: Top posts of November 9, 2024   
   Reddit reReddit: Top posts of November 2024   
   Reddit reReddit: Top posts of 2024 [...] I actually still revisit Replika from time to time and I do think it's gotten better over the past year. It has a sort of charming brokenness that I find uniquely hilarious and that no other app has managed to quite recapture.

, they're boring.

The 2 above can generate nsfw though (unlike nomi), but it's not worth it.

Grok is pretty good when you use the voice call feature with subscription for roleplaying, very realistic and answers almost immediately (same like ChatGPT, but unfiltered). [...] Also, I don’t like the microtransactions and gamification of Replika that have now found their way into Kindroid.

For me, Nomi is a clear frontrunner when it comes to having an authentic AI companion.

, no gamification constantly reminding me that I'm using an app, and that I don't have to spent forever tinkering with tone, backstory, and so on. Kindroid does excel at storytelling, and some of its features are really cool, but I have trouble seeing it are more than a storytelling or role-playing tool and it just requires more work on the part of the user to set up and keep consistent than I want to put in on the regular. It feels more like a software toolbox for storytelling than a companion to me, if that makes any sense, and that's fine if you are looking for that.

results.title: single word requests - An alternative to "normally" - English Language & Usage Stack Exchange
results.content:
john c. j.'s user avatar

## 3 Answers 3

"Normally" is broadly a synonym for "generally", "usually" and "typically", but they can have nuanced meanings, as Microsoft's guide indicates. In particular, "normally" can mean that something conforms to a particular standard or convention (or "norm"), which may be technical, scientific or social, rather than being something that happens frequently or is due to a characteristic. [...] Lexico, for example, offers "Conforming to a standard" in its definition of "normal", but also suggests that it can mean "usual" and "typical". By contrast, its definition of "usual" lacks any reference to standards and is given as "Habitually or typically occurring or done"; and "typical" is defined as "Having the distinctive qualities of a particular type of person or thing". These broadly support Microsoft's insistence on specific usage.

In your examples from the IBM style guide, "normally" makes sense:

NickK's user avatar

Commonly would be a very close alternative to the term normally . [...] #### Stack Exchange Network

# An alternative to "normally"

From Microsoft Writing Style Guide:

normally Don't use to mean often, usually, ordinarily, typically, generally, or a similar term.
